findings.description: Precedence is inverted: module/load docstrings promise 'env > file > defaults', but `merged.update(self._read_file())` runs AFTER `_read_env()`, so dict.update makes the FILE win over env vars — environment overrides are silently ignored whenever the key also exists in the YAML file, defeating the module's entire stated purpose.

`yaml.load(f)` is called without a `Loader=` argument (noted in passing; defer to critic-security): on PyYAML <5.1 this is an arbitrary-object-construction/RCE sink, and on PyYAML >=5.1 it raises TypeError/warns, so config loading either is exploitable or crashes on every read depending on installed version — use `yaml.safe_load`.

`feature_enabled` does `self.get('feature_flags', {})` then `flags.get(flag, ...)`, but the `{}` fallback only triggers when the key is ABSENT; if `feature_flags` is overridden via env (`APP_FEATURE_FLAGS=...`) it is coerced to a scalar str/int/bool which has no `.get`, raising AttributeError instead of degrading gracefully.

`merged = dict(DEFAULTS)` is a shallow copy, so `merged['feature_flags']` aliases the module-level `DEFAULTS['feature_flags']` dict; if a caller mutates the returned config's feature_flags (when not overridden by file/env) it corrupts the shared global default for all subsequent loads.

`get`, `require`, and `feature_enabled` each call `load()`, which re-reads/re-parses the YAML file and re-scans os.environ on every single-key access (defer perf detail to critic-performance); the logic consequence is non-idempotence — INFO 'config loaded' is logged on every get() (line 69) and file-system state changes between calls yield inconsistent values within one logical operation.

`require` treats `value is None` as 'missing', but a key explicitly set to YAML `null` exists yet returns None, so it is wrongly reported as missing; it also cannot distinguish an absent key from one legitimately set to None, producing a misleading KeyError.

`_coerce` unconditionally and lossily converts any numeric-looking env value to int/float with no opt-out, so string-typed config (e.g. `APP_LOG_LEVEL=0`, zip codes, version/account ids, `1_000` underscore literals, `1e3`) is silently retyped, breaking call sites that expect strings.

`_coerce` accepts `float('inf')`, `'-inf'`, and `'nan'` as valid floats, so `APP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_S=nan` or `=inf` silently produces NaN/Infinity that poisons downstream timeout/arithmetic comparisons.

Because `_coerce` only treats exact 'true'/'false' as bool, a flag value like 'off'/'no'/'disabled' stays a non-empty string and `bool(flags.get(flag))` evaluates it as True, so feature_enabled returns True for strings intended to mean disabled.
An empty env value (`APP_DB_URL=`) yields `raw=''` which fails int()/float() and is returned as '', silently overriding a meaningful default with an empty string with no validation.
